originalTitle: The cash-in-the-sofa saga that just won't go away for South Africa's president
title: The cash-in-the-sofa saga that just won't go away for South Africa's president
content: A committee of MPs has been formed to look at the case against Cyril Ramaphosa, but he may survive a vote.

originalTitle: Nearly 50 people die of thirst in Sahara desert after lorry breaks down
title: A Big Adventure, A Brave Rescue, and Why Water is Super Important!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Imagine a place so incredibly vast and hot, it feels like the sun gives it a big, warm hug all day long! That's a bit like the Sahara Desert, one of the most amazing and biggest deserts in our wonderful continent, Africa. It's a place of incredible beauty, but also a place where you need to be very careful.

A Long Journey Begins

Recently, some people were going on a very long journey across this big desert. They were travelling in a large truck, like a lorry, with many people inside. Maybe they were going to see family, or to find new places. Journeys in the desert can take a very long time, and you have to be ready for anything.

Oh No! The Lorry Breaks Down!

One day, something went wrong. The big lorry that was carrying everyone suddenly broke down. It stopped working, just like when your favourite toy car runs out of batteries or gets a flat tyre. But in the middle of a huge desert, far from towns and roads, a broken-down lorry is a really, really big problem. There were no mechanics nearby, no shops to buy new parts, and no easy way to call for help.

The Big Walk for Help

Without their truck, the people knew they were in trouble. They needed water, and they needed to find help. So, some of the brave people decided they had to walk. They started walking to find someone who could rescue them and their friends. They walked a super, super long way – more than 50 kilometres! To help you imagine, 50 kilometres is like walking from your home to a town very far away, many, many times over. It's a distance that takes many hours, or even days, to walk. Imagine how tired your legs would be!

The Desert is Very Thirsty

The desert is a very dry place. It doesn't have many rivers, lakes, or water taps where you can easily get a drink. So, as the days went by and the people kept walking, they became very, very thirsty. Water is like magic fuel for our bodies. Without enough water, our bodies get tired, feel dizzy, and can get sick very quickly. This is why it's so important to always drink water, especially when you are playing outside or when it's hot. Your body needs water to help you play, learn, and grow strong!

Brave Hearts Keep Going

From the group that started walking, two people were incredibly brave and strong. Even though they were so tired and so, so thirsty, they kept going. They knew they had to find help for everyone. They walked for days and days, never giving up hope, trying to find another person or a village. They showed amazing courage, pushing themselves through the hot sun and the cold desert nights.

Finding Help at Last!

Finally, after walking for what seemed like forever, these two brave people found other kind people. Imagine their relief! They told the people what had happened to their friends and family who were still stuck back with the broken lorry in the middle of the desert. Quickly, the helpful people called for rescue teams to go out and search.

A Sad Reminder

Rescue teams rushed into the desert to try and find everyone. But because the Sahara Desert is so unbelievably huge, and it had been many, many days since the lorry broke down, it was a very difficult search. Sadly, many of the other people who were with them couldn't make it. They became too thirsty and too sick in the big, hot, dry desert. This is a very sad part of the story, but it reminds us how truly, truly important water is for every single person and every living thing on Earth. It teaches us a very important lesson about staying safe.

Why Water is So Super Important

Every single one of us needs water to live. We drink water to keep our bodies working, to help us play games, learn new things at school, and grow big and strong. When you run around and play, you might feel thirsty, right? That's your body telling you, "Hey! I need some water, please!" Always listen to your body and drink water often!

Did You Know? Fun Facts!

* Did you know? The Sahara Desert is the biggest hot desert in the entire world! It's so big that it's nearly the same size as the whole country of China! Imagine how much sand that is! * Did you know? A special animal called a camel is a desert superhero! Camels can walk for many, many days in the desert without drinking water because they have a special way of storing water in their bodies. * Did you know? Even when you don't feel super thirsty, it's a good idea to drink water throughout the day. It helps your brain think clearly and keeps you full of energy!

Lessons from the Desert

This story, even though it has a sad part, teaches us some very important things. First, always make sure you have enough water when you go on a trip, especially to a hot or dry place. Second, always listen to grown-ups! They know how to plan safely and keep you out of danger. It also shows us how incredibly brave people can be when they are trying to help others. The two people who walked so far were trying to save their friends and family. That's one of the kindest and bravest things anyone can do.

Africa is Amazing and Strong!

Our continent, Africa, is full of amazing places, wonderful animals, and incredibly strong people. Even though sometimes difficult things happen, people in Africa always try their best to help each other, learn from challenges, and stay strong together. We learn from these tough times to be even safer and kinder. So, next time you are playing outside or going on a trip, remember to drink your water! It's your body's best friend. And always tell a grown-up where you are going and stay close to them. Staying safe is super cool!
summary: In the vast Sahara Desert, a lorry broke down, leaving many people without water; two brave survivors walked a long distance to get help, reminding us all how precious water is and why safety is so important.
whyMatters: This story matters because it shows us how important water is for everyone to live, how brave people can be when they help each other, and why we always need to be careful and prepared when we travel, especially in big, wild places like the desert.
